claimText: Ramjet engines operate at Mach 3 to 6 with subsonic combustion, whereas Scramjet engines operate above Mach 6 with supersonic combustion.
evidenceExcerpt: Ramjet engines operate at Mach 3 to 6 with subsonic combustion, while Scramjet engines operate above Mach 6 with supersonic combustion.
examUse: Statement-based question comparing Ramjet and Scramjet engine characteristics.
distractorHooks: Swapping subsonic and supersonic combustion between Ramjet and Scramjet

claimText: Both Ramjet and Scramjet engines cannot produce thrust at zero airspeed and require assisted take-off.
evidenceExcerpt: Both Ramjets and Scramjets require assisted take-off (e.g., by a rocket) as they cannot produce thrust at zero airspeed.
examUse: Statement-based question on the operational limitations of air-breathing engines.
distractorHooks: Claiming Scramjets can take off independently from a standstill
Stating only Ramjets require assisted take-off

claimText: Scramjet engines are more efficient than traditional rockets because they utilize atmospheric oxygen for combustion rather than carrying an onboard oxidizer.
evidenceExcerpt: Scramjets are more efficient than rockets because they use atmospheric oxygen for combustion instead of carrying an oxidizer.
examUse: Reason-Assertion question on why air-breathing engines are preferred for certain flight profiles.
distractorHooks: Stating Scramjets carry their own oxidizer

claimText: Hypersonic weapons travel at speeds of Mach 5 or faster and fly at lower altitudes compared to ballistic missiles, making them more difficult to track.
evidenceExcerpt: Hypersonic weapons travel at Mach 5 or faster and fly at lower altitudes than ballistic missiles, making them harder to track.
examUse: Statement-based question on the advantages and flight profile of hypersonic weapons.
distractorHooks: Claiming hypersonic weapons fly at higher altitudes than ballistic missiles
Stating hypersonic weapons travel below Mach 5

claimText: Hypersonic Glide Vehicles (HGV) glide to their targets after being launched from rockets, whereas Hypersonic Cruise Missiles (HCM) are powered throughout their flight by Scramjet engines.
evidenceExcerpt: Hypersonic Glide Vehicles (HGV) are launched from rockets and glide to targets, whereas Hypersonic Cruise Missiles (HCM) are powered throughout flight by Scramjet engines.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question distinguishing between types of hypersonic weapons.
distractorHooks: Swapping the definitions of HGV and HCM
Claiming HGVs are powered by Scramjets throughout their flight
